andrew-authority.bestFor: High-ticket financial VSLs, proof-led content, trust-building
andrew-authority.notes: Top pick for $25K-$100K products. Warm confidence without hype.

christopher-formal.notes: Backup if Andrew sounds too casual. More formal authority.

guy-energetic.notes: Wildcard. Slowed down and deepened to prevent over-animation.

research.genderRationale: Male voice for high-ticket finance: authority + identification with target avatar (male, 35-55, $150K-$400K income)
research.accentRationale: Standard American English — clean, educated, un-regional (Bloomberg/NPR tone)
research.ageRationale: 35-50 voice: successful + modern. Not too young (lacks authority) or old (dated for AI positioning)
research.paceRationale: 140-155 WPM average. Slow to 130 for data reveals. Brisk to 155 for objections. Never above 160.
research.toneRationale: Calm authority > urgent > warm. Private briefing from a confident insider.
sources: Harris Interactive: 48% say male voices sound 'more forceful'
Stansberry/Agora pattern: overwhelmingly male narrators for financial VSLs
1991 persuasion study: slower speech more persuasive when audience already agrees
Smith & Shaffer 1976: faster speakers rated 30% more competent in business contexts
